누가복음 2:1.content: Now in those days, a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that all the world should be enrolled.

누가복음 2:2.content: This was the first enrollment made when Quirinius was governor of Syria.

누가복음 2:3.content: All went to enroll themselves, everyone to his own city.

누가복음 2:4.content: Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth, into Judea, to David’s city, which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and family of David;

누가복음 2:5.content: to enroll himself with Mary, who was pledged to be married to him as wife, being pregnant.

누가복음 2:6.content: While they were there, the day had come for her to give birth.

누가복음 2:7.content: She gave birth to her firstborn son. She wrapped him in bands of cloth, and laid him in a feeding trough, because there was no room for them in the inn.

누가복음 2:8.content: There were shepherds in the same country staying in the field, and keeping watch by night over their flock.

누가복음 2:9.content: Behold, an angel of the Lord stood by them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.

누가복음 2:10.content: The angel said to them, “Don’t be afraid, for behold, I bring you good news of great joy which will be to all the people.

누가복음 2:11.content: For there is born to you today, in David’s city, a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.

누가복음 2:12.content: This is the sign to you: you will find a baby wrapped in strips of cloth, lying in a feeding trough.”

누가복음 2:13.content: Suddenly, there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly army praising God, and saying,

누가복음 2:14.content: “Glory to God in the highest, on earth peace, good will toward men.”

누가복음 2:15.content: When the angels went away from them into the sky, the shepherds said to one another, “Let’s go to Bethlehem, now, and see this thing that has happened, which the Lord has made known to us.”

누가복음 2:16.content: They came with haste, and found both Mary and Joseph, and the baby was lying in the feeding trough.

누가복음 2:17.content: When they saw it, they publicized widely the saying which was spoken to them about this child.

누가복음 2:18.content: All who heard it wondered at the things which were spoken to them by the shepherds.

누가복음 2:19.content: But Mary kept all these sayings, pondering them in her heart.

누가복음 2:20.content: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things that they had heard and seen, just as it was told them.

누가복음 2:21.content: When eight days were fulfilled for the circumcision of the child, his name was called Jesus, which was given by the angel before he was conceived in the womb.

누가복음 2:22.content: When the days of their purification according to the law of Moses were fulfilled, they brought him up to Jerusalem, to present him to the Lord

누가복음 2:23.content: (as it is written in the law of the Lord, “Every male who opens the womb shall be called holy to the Lord”),

누가복음 2:24.content: and to offer a sacrifice according to that which is said in the law of the Lord, “A pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons.”

누가복음 2:25.content: Behold, there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon. This man was righteous and devout, looking for the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was on him.

누가복음 2:26.content: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he should not see death before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.

누가복음 2:27.content: He came in the Spirit into the temple. When the parents brought in the child, Jesus, that they might do concerning him according to the custom of the law,

누가복음 2:28.content: then he received him into his arms, and blessed God, and said,

누가복음 2:29.content: “Now you are releasing your servant, Master, according to your word, in peace;

누가복음 2:30.content: for my eyes have seen your salvation,

누가복음 2:31.content: which you have prepared before the face of all peoples;

누가복음 2:32.content: a light for revelation to the nations, and the glory of your people Israel.”

누가복음 2:33.content: Joseph and his mother were marveling at the things which were spoken concerning him,

누가복음 2:34.content: and Simeon blessed them, and said to Mary, his mother, “Behold, this child is set for the falling and the rising of many in Israel, and for a sign which is spoken against.

누가복음 2:35.content: Yes, a sword will pierce through your own soul, that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed.”

누가복음 2:36.content: There was one Anna, a prophetess, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher (she was of a great age, having lived with a husband seven years from her virginity,

누가복음 2:37.content: and she had been a widow for about eighty-four years), who didn’t depart from the temple, worshiping with fastings and petitions night and day.

누가복음 2:38.content: Coming up at that very hour, she gave thanks to the Lord, and spoke of him to all those who were looking for redemption in Jerusalem.

누가복음 2:39.content: When they had accomplished all things that were according to the law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, to their own city, Nazareth.

누가복음 2:40.content: The child was growing, and was becoming strong in spirit, being filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was upon him.

누가복음 2:41.content: His parents went every year to Jerusalem at the feast of the Passover.

누가복음 2:42.content: When he was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to the custom of the feast,

누가복음 2:43.content: and when they had fulfilled the days, as they were returning, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. Joseph and his mother didn’t know it,

누가복음 2:44.content: but supposing him to be in the company, they went a day’s journey, and they looked for him among their relatives and acquaintances.

누가복음 2:45.content: When they didn’t find him, they returned to Jerusalem, looking for him.

누가복음 2:46.content: After three days they found him in the temple, sitting in the middle of the teachers, both listening to them, and asking them questions.

누가복음 2:47.content: All who heard him were amazed at his understanding and his answers.

누가복음 2:48.content: When they saw him, they were astonished, and his mother said to him, “Son, why have you treated us this way? Behold, your father and I were anxiously looking for you.”

누가복음 2:49.content: He said to them, “Why were you looking for me? Didn’t you know that I must be in my Father’s house?”

누가복음 2:50.content: They didn’t understand the saying which he spoke to them.

누가복음 2:51.content: And he went down with them, and came to Nazareth. He was subject to them, and his mother kept all these sayings in her heart.

누가복음 2:52.content: And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and men.
